faqs.question: Why is Mumbai called the 'City of Dreams'?
faqs.answer: Mumbai is called the City of Dreams because people from all over India come here hoping to find good jobs, better education, and a chance to succeed. It is home to Bollywood (Hindi film industry), big businesses, and many opportunities. People believe that if they work hard in Mumbai, their dreams can come true!

faqs.question: How many people live in Mumbai?
faqs.answer: Mumbai has over 2 crore (20 million) people living in it! That makes it the most populated city in India. So many people live in Mumbai that the local trains, roads, and buses are always very crowded, especially during morning and evening hours when people travel to and from work.

faqs.question: What is the difference between a village and a city?
faqs.answer: A village has open fields, fewer people, fresh air, and people mostly do farming. Homes are often on the ground with gardens. A city has tall buildings, many vehicles, factories, offices, and millions of people. Villages are quiet and green, while cities are busy and noisy. Both have their own advantages! Villages have fresh food and nature, cities have more schools and hospitals.

faqs.question: What is a chawl and why is it special in Mumbai?
faqs.answer: A chawl is an old-style building in Mumbai where many families live close together. Each family usually has one or two small rooms, and everyone shares the corridor, staircase, water taps, and sometimes even bathrooms. Chawls are special because the families become like one big family - children play together, neighbours share food during festivals, and everyone helps each other. Some famous chawls are over 100 years old!

faqs.question: Who are dabbawalas and how do they deliver so many lunch boxes?
faqs.answer: Dabbawalas are delivery people in Mumbai who pick up home-cooked lunch boxes (called dabbas or tiffins) from homes and deliver them to offices across the city using local trains and bicycles. About 5,000 dabbawalas deliver around 2 lakh dabbas every day with hardly any mistakes! They use a special colour and number coding system on each dabba. They are so accurate that even big universities have studied their system!

faqs.question: Why do some people in cities live in slums?
faqs.answer: When people come to a big city looking for work, they sometimes cannot afford proper houses because city homes are very expensive. So they build small shelters using tin sheets, plastic, and wood in empty spaces. These areas are called slums. Slum families often face problems like no clean water, no proper toilets, and crowded spaces. The government tries to provide better housing, water, and schools in slum areas.

faqs.question: Why is there so much traffic in cities?
faqs.answer: Cities have traffic jams because millions of people need to go to work, school, and markets at the same time. There are too many cars, buses, trucks, and auto-rickshaws on roads that are not wide enough. That is why cities build metro trains and flyovers, and encourage people to use public transport like buses and trains instead of their own cars. Walking and cycling also help reduce traffic!

faqs.question: What is Marine Drive and why is it called the 'Queen's Necklace'?
faqs.answer: Marine Drive is a beautiful curved road along the coast of Mumbai facing the Arabian Sea. People love to walk there in the evening and enjoy the sea breeze. At night, when all the street lights along the curved road switch on, they look like a string of pearls or a sparkling necklace from above. That is why it is called the Queen's Necklace!

faqs.question: What food is Mumbai famous for?
faqs.answer: Mumbai is famous for vada pav (a spicy potato patty in bread, often called Mumbai's burger), pav bhaji (mixed vegetable curry with buttered bread), bhel puri (puffed rice snack), and cutting chai (half a cup of strong sweet tea). You can find street food stalls all over the city. Mumbai's food is a mix of flavours from different parts of India because so many communities live there!

faqs.question: What happens in Mumbai during heavy rains?
faqs.answer: Mumbai gets very heavy rainfall during the monsoon (June to September). Sometimes the rain is so heavy that streets get flooded with water, local trains stop running, and people get stuck. Mumbai was built on islands and is close to sea level, so water does not drain easily. Despite the flooding, the city's spirit stays strong - people help each other and get back to normal quickly. Mumbaikars (people of Mumbai) are known for their resilience!
